prose:
Method–provenance block
Property: Fukui reactivity indices (f+(r), f−(r), f0(r); dual descriptor) for phenol; headline value = maximum electrophilic Fukui function f+ on atom 3 (C)
Level of theory: DFT, tier “standard” ≡ B3LYP/def2-TZVP with density fitting (PySCF)
Geometry provenance: single-point calculations on the supplied file /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/phenol/phenol.xyz (no further optimisation)
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent)
Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet (0, 1) for N calculation; cation (+1, 2) and anion (−1, 2) for N ± 1 states
Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (backend PySCF 2.x; ASE wrappers for charge/mult runs)
Convergence & caveats: all three single-point SCFs converged. Integrity check status: ok; trustworthy = true.
Warnings (verbatim):
• sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• common header present
Live .out log: written during execution alongside the result JSON (same directory as …/live.json) under the standard chemkit naming scheme (fukui_YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.out); available for inspection.

Key numerical results (e):
– Maximum electrophilic Fukui index f+  = 2.1028319 on atom 3 (C, 0-based index 2)
– Maximum nucleophilic Fukui index f−  = 0.16201477 on atom 1 (O, 0-based index 0)
– Dual descriptor indicates C3 is the most electrophilic site and O1 is the most nucleophilic.

All numbers are reported exactly as returned by the chemkit fukui-reactivity run; no rounding applied.
